steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure around the blank
steps.content: Before the blank: had as many as 20,000 inhabitants in the year 1150 CE completes a main clause about Cahokia's population. After the blank: making it one of the largest cities in North America at the time is a modifier phrase built on the participle “making” (the -ing verb form used as a modifier, not a main verb).

steps.title: Apply the comma-before-modifier rule
steps.content:
A comma separates a main clause from a modifier phrase that follows it, properly connecting the population fact to its consequence. The period (B) would create a sentence fragment; no punctuation (A) would fuse two structural elements; the semicolon (D) connects only independent clauses, not a clause and a modifier phrase.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
